Title: A chromosome-level genome assembly and multi-omics analyses provide insights into the biosynthesis of piperlongumine in Piper sarmentosum

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase family; HXXXD and DFGWG motifs
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal amide-forming branch of piperlongumine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Proposed to catalyze condensation of sinapoyl-CoA and 5,6-dihydropyridin-2(1H)-one to form the amide precursor of piperlongumine
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key candidate enzyme; hub gene in PL-associated module
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: anticancer; anti-inflammatory; antidiabetic; antidepressant; neuroprotective; cardiovascular protective
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes piperlongumine biosynthesis as a proposed key precursor-forming enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA hub-gene identification; transcript-metabolite correlation; tissue expression analysis; subcellular localization in tobacco leaves; AlphaFold-based structure modeling; molecular docking with substrates and product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsHCT1 was the most highly connected hub gene in the PL-associated Darkgreen coexpression module. 2. It showed high expression in flowers and fruits, the main organs for PL accumulation. 3. Molecular docking supported binding to sinapoyl-CoA, 5,6-dihydropyridin-2(1H)-one, and predicted product, supporting a role in amide precursor formation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in flowers and fruits; highest among BADH family members
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: natural tissue/developmental comparison across root, stem, leaf, flower, and fruit
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA1289360); untargeted metabolomics (MetaboLights MTBLS13523); WGCNA; genome assembly/annotation (PRJNA1265616)
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter used for transient subcellular localization assay in tobacco
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising candidate for metabolic engineering or synthetic biology to enhance piperlongumine production; should be prioritized for functional validation and possible stacking with downstream methyltransferase genes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function is proposed from coexpression and docking, and the paper states further experimental validation is still required.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Final methylation step of piperlongumine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Proposed to methylate the piperlongumine precursor by adding a methoxy group to generate piperlongumine
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key candidate enzyme for final synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: anticancer; anti-inflammatory; antidiabetic; antidepressant; neuroprotective; cardiovascular protective
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes final synthesis of piperlongumine as the proposed terminal O-methyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene family analysis; high tissue expression; molecular docking with PL precursor and PL; transcript-metabolite integration
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsCCoAOMT1 was the highest-expressed member of the PsCCoAOMT subfamily. 2. Docking showed strong binding to PL precursor and PL, consistent with a methylation role. 3. Integrated multi-omics analysis identified it as a key gene for final PL synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly expressed; highest expression among PsCCoAOMT subfamily members
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in PL-associated tissues, especially flowers and fruits as inferred from PL biosynthesis context
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: natural tissue/developmental comparison across root, stem, leaf, flower, and fruit
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA1289360); untargeted metabolomics (MetaboLights MTBLS13523); genome assembly/annotation (PRJNA1265616)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream engineering target for raising PL content, especially in combination with precursor-supplying and amide-forming enzymes such as PsHCT1
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assignment is based on expression, phylogenetic classification, and docking; the paper notes additional experimental validation is needed.
